title: Gel electrophoresis
steps: For 200 mL of a 1.2% agarose gel, add 2.4 g of agarose to 200 mL TAE or TBE in a conical flask and mix by swirling the flask.
Microwave on a high power for about 00:03:00 , or until boiling, swirling the flask approximately every 00:00:30 during heating to ensure all the agarose dissolves. Use heat proof gloves.
Add 10 µL SafeView stain to the hot agarose and swirl to mix.
Allow the agarose to cool until you are able to comfortably touch the outside of the flask (approximately 00:05:00 ).
Pour the agarose into the casting tray. Pop any bubbles or move them to the edges of the mould using a pipette tip. Place the comb as desired.
Place the gel (still in the casting tray) gently into the tank, ensuring there is enough TAE or TBE running buffer to cover it.
On a piece of parafilm, dot 1 µL of 6x loading dye using a pipette.
Pipette 5 µL ladder or 6 µL PCR product directly onto the loading dye and mix by pipetting up and down several times.
Place the lid on the tank, making sure that the wells are at the negative electrode end of the tank. The samples should run from black to red (negative to positive).
If the buffer used for PCR already contains loading dye, skip to step 11.
Allow the gel to set for approximately 00:40:00 .
Set the power supply to between 80-120V , depending on the expected amplicon size and desired duration of electrophoresis.

Run the electrophoresis until the visible dye bands are about 75% of the way down the gel, approximately 1-1.5 hours. Once complete, ensure you disconnect the power supply from the gel tank, before removing the lid.
creator: Victoria LN Jackson
